data.description:
## Story C: Test-gate recording scripts consume `commands.test_runner` (replace hardcoded pytest)

### Description
**What**: Update the test-gate recording script (hardcoded `python3 -m pytest`) and the test-gate exemption script (hardcoded `python3 -m pytest "$NODE_ID"`) to read `commands.test_runner` and invoke it with the target test path. This is the behavioral payoff — Ruby and JS projects get real test-gate signal from this story.
**Why**: Today `.rb` and `.js`/`.ts` files produce no test-gate signal because the recording script skips the hardcoded pytest call for non-Python files. Ruby and JS projects appear to pass the gate while their tests are never actually run.
**Scope**:
- IN: both hardcoded pytest call sites replaced with config-key reads, exit-code propagation preserved (0=pass, non-zero=fail), centrality-aware tier mapping unchanged
- OUT: per-file gate (Story E), reinstall-hooks.sh precondition change (Story D), config-absent warning path (covered by library helper if needed)

### Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, neither script contains a hardcoded `python3 -m pytest` invocation
  ← Satisfies: "record-test-status.sh routes .rb, .js, .ts files to commands.test_runner instead of hardcoded python3/pytest" + "record-test-exemption.sh uses commands.test_runner instead of hardcoded python3 -m pytest"
- When this story is complete, recording on a `.rb` source file in a Ruby-detected project invokes `$commands.test_runner` and captures its exit code as the test result
  ← Satisfies: "record-test-status.sh routes .rb, .js, .ts files to commands.test_runner"
- When this story is complete, the recording script emits an actionable `[DSO WARN]` when `commands.test_runner` is missing at gate execution time and skips (does not silently pass)
  ← Satisfies: epic Scenario Analysis "commands.test_runner absent at gate execution time"

### Considerations
- [Testing] Mock `commands.test_runner` in unit tests; do NOT invoke real pytest/rspec/jest
- [Reliability] Exit code 0/non-zero contract identical across pytest/RSpec/Jest (confirmed in epic Research Findings); no per-runner special-casing required
- [Testing] Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a Ruby or JS developer, my test-gate recording runs via commands.test_runner (not hardcoded pytest)
